OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Apr-27-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Plaintiffss Motion To Vacate Judgment [C.C.P. 473]
Off Calendar Aug-27-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARRIANNE B. SIOSON OF THE LAW OFFICES OF BRIAN L. LARSEN, TEL. NO. 1-415-398-5000 APPERING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF FRANK CHU'S Motion To Vacate Judgment [C.C.P. 473] is GRANTED. The general underlying purpose of Section 473(b) is to promote the determination of actions on their merits and avoid precipitating more litigation in the form of malpractice suits. (Even Zohar Const. & Remodeling, Inc. v. Bellaire Townhouses, LLC (2015) 61 Cal.4th 830, 838-39.) "[C]alendar errors by an attorney or a member of his staff are, under appropriate circumstances, excusable." (Nilsson v. City of Los Angeles (1967) 249 Cal.App.2d 976, 980.) Furthermore, "California courts are divided as to whether the mandatory relief provision of Code of Civil Procedure Section 473(b) applies in a context other than default." (Hossain v. Hossain (2007) 157 Cal.App.4th 454, 457.) This Court holds that Plaintiff is entitled to discretionary relief under Section 473(b). Plaintiff's counsel accepts fault and responsibility for the entry of judgment in this case. There is no showing that plaintiffs bore any responsibility nor is there a showing that defendants would suffer prejudice if this matter proceeded to trial. Order signed in open court. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/sb

ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-10-2014 Continued To May-19-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Reclassify Action To Court Of Unlimited Jurisdiction; And Motion To Reclassify
Off Calendar Oct-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARRIANNE B. SIOSON OF THE LAW OFFICES OF BRIAN L. LARSEN, TEL. NO. 398-5000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RYAN L. KRAFT OF THE STRATMAN, PATTERSON & HUNTER, TEL. NO. (510)457-3483 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF FRANK CHU's Motion To Reclassify Action To Court Of Unlimited Jurisdiction is Granted. A motion to reclassify from a limited to an unlimited case must be granted unless it appears to a legal certainty that plaintiff's damages will necessarily be $25,000 or less. (Ytuarte v. Sup. Ct. (2005) 129 Cal.App.4th 266, 279, "the court should reject the plaintiff's effort to reclassify the action as unlimited only when the lack of jurisdiction as an 'unlimited' case is certain and clear.") Plaintiff submits evidence that he underwent neck surgery to alleviate arm pain. Plaintiff may be able to establish through expert testimony that the neck surgery was attributable, at least in part, to the subject car accident. Therefore, it is not a legal certainty that Plaintiff will be unable to recover more than $25,000. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
